#!/usr/bin/env bash
# scripts/benchmark/benchmark — operator-facing benchmark driver.
#
# Replaces `fiz-bench sweep` / `fiz-bench matrix` execution. Pure shell:
# yq + jq + sha256sum + docker. No Go bridge, no Python.
#
# Bead fizeau-fe87a3ac: skeleton + listing subcommands + --plan mode +
# preflight rebuild gate. The cell execution loop (resume, retry, signal
# handling, concurrency, budget) lands in a follow-up bead.
#
# Operator contract:
#   ./benchmark profiles
#   ./benchmark bench-sets
#   ./benchmark harness-adapters
#   ./benchmark task-executors
#   ./benchmark validate                            # placeholder; see PR C
#   ./benchmark preflight                           # rebuild harbor-runner if sha drifted
#   ./benchmark --profile P[,P2,...] --bench-set B[,B2,...] --plan
#   ./benchmark --profile P[,P2,...] --bench-set B[,B2,...]   # run path; PR A4
#
# Rules:
#   1. --plan is pure: no file writes, no Docker pulls/builds, no
#      benchmark-results/ creation. Prints one matrix line per cell.
#   2. --profile and --bench-set are both required for --plan and run.
#   3. preflight is the only command that touches Docker.

set -euo pipefail

SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
PROFILES_DIR="${PROFILES_DIR:-${SCRIPT_DIR}/profiles}"
BENCH_SETS_DIR="${BENCH_SETS_DIR:-${SCRIPT_DIR}/bench-sets}"
HARNESS_ADAPTERS_DIR="${HARNESS_ADAPTERS_DIR:-${SCRIPT_DIR}/harness-adapters}"
TASK_EXECUTORS_DIR="${TASK_EXECUTORS_DIR:-${SCRIPT_DIR}/task-executors}"
HARBOR_BUILD_SCRIPT="${SCRIPT_DIR}/build-harbor-runner.sh"
HARBOR_IMAGE_TAG="${HARBOR_IMAGE_TAG:-fizeau-harbor-runner:latest}"
DEFAULT_OUT_ROOT="${DEFAULT_OUT_ROOT:-bench/results/fiz-tools-v1}"
BENCH_TASKS_DIR_DEFAULT="${SCRIPT_DIR}/external/terminal-bench-2/tasks"
BENCH_RETRY_MAX_ATTEMPTS="${BENCH_RETRY_MAX_ATTEMPTS:-4}"
BENCH_RETRY_BACKOFF_BASE="${BENCH_RETRY_BACKOFF_BASE:-1}"
FIZEAU_BENCH_STATE_DIR="${FIZEAU_BENCH_STATE_DIR:-${XDG_CACHE_HOME:-$HOME/.cache}/fizeau-benchmark}"
BENCH_TERM_GRACE_SECONDS="${BENCH_TERM_GRACE_SECONDS:-30}"

die() { printf 'benchmark: %s\n' "$*" >&2; exit 1; }
warn() { printf 'benchmark: %s\n' "$*" >&2; }

require_tool() {
  command -v "$1" >/dev/null 2>&1 || die "required tool not found: $1"
}

# in_flight_state_dir returns the host-specific state directory.
in_flight_state_dir() {
  local hostname
  hostname="$(hostname)"
  printf '%s/%s\n' "${FIZEAU_BENCH_STATE_DIR}" "${hostname}"
}

# in_flight_lock_path returns the lock path for a concurrency group.
in_flight_lock_path() {
  local group="$1"
  printf '%s/locks/%s.lock\n' "${FIZEAU_BENCH_STATE_DIR}" "${group}"
}

# in_flight_json_path returns the path to the in-flight state JSON file.
in_flight_json_path() {
  printf '%s/in-flight.json\n' "$(in_flight_state_dir)"
}

# acquire_group_lock acquires an flock for a concurrency group, blocking if needed.
# Returns 0 on success, prints the lock fd to stdout.
acquire_group_lock() {
  local group="$1"
  local lock_path
  lock_path="$(in_flight_lock_path "${group}")"
  mkdir -p "$(dirname "${lock_path}")"
  local lock_fd
  lock_fd=200
  eval "exec ${lock_fd}>'${lock_path}'"
  flock "${lock_fd}" || die "failed to acquire lock for concurrency-group=${group}"
  printf '%d\n' "${lock_fd}"
}

# release_group_lock releases an flock acquired by acquire_group_lock.
release_group_lock() {
  local lock_fd="$1"
  eval "exec ${lock_fd}>&-" || true
}

# register_inflight registers a cell as in-flight in the state file.
# Reads current entries, adds this cell, removes dead PIDs, writes back.
register_inflight() {
  local cell_id="$1" cell_dir="$2"
  local state_dir
  state_dir="$(in_flight_state_dir)"
  mkdir -p "${state_dir}"
  local json_path
  json_path="$(in_flight_json_path)"

  local current_json="{\"cells\":[]}"
  [[ -f "${json_path}" ]] && current_json="$(cat "${json_path}")" || true

  # Prune dead PIDs by filtering in bash
  local live_cells=()
  local cell_json pid
  while IFS= read -r cell_json; do
    [[ -z "${cell_json}" ]] && continue
    pid="$(jq -r '.pid' <<<"${cell_json}")"
    if [[ -n "${pid}" ]] && kill -0 "${pid}" 2>/dev/null; then
      live_cells+=("${cell_json}")
    fi
  done < <(jq -r '.cells[] | @json' <<<"${current_json}")

  # Rebuild with live cells
  current_json="$(printf '%s\n' "${live_cells[@]}" | jq -s '{cells:.}')"

  # Add this cell
  current_json="$(jq \
    --arg cell_id "${cell_id}" \
    --arg cell_dir "${cell_dir}" \
    --argjson pid $$ \
    '.cells += [{cell_id:$cell_id, cell_dir:$cell_dir, pid:$pid}]' \
    <<<"${current_json}")"

  printf '%s\n' "${current_json}" >"${json_path}.tmp"
  mv "${json_path}.tmp" "${json_path}"
}


# unregister_inflight removes a cell from the in-flight state file.
unregister_inflight() {
  local cell_id="$1"
  local json_path
  json_path="$(in_flight_json_path)"
  [[ -f "${json_path}" ]] || return 0

  local updated
  updated="$(jq \
    --arg cell_id "${cell_id}" \
    '.cells |= map(select(.cell_id != $cell_id))' \
    "${json_path}")"

  printf '%s\n' "${updated}" >"${json_path}.tmp"
  mv "${json_path}.tmp" "${json_path}"
}

# interrupt_inflight_cells is called during signal handling to mark in-flight
# cells as interrupted and clean them up from the JSON file.
interrupt_inflight_cells() {
  local json_path
  json_path="$(in_flight_json_path)"
  [[ -f "${json_path}" ]] || return 0

  local cells
  cells="$(jq -r '.cells[] | @json' "${json_path}")" || return 0

  local cell_id cell_dir pid
  while IFS= read -r cell_json; do
    [[ -z "${cell_json}" ]] && continue
    cell_id="$(jq -r '.cell_id' <<<"${cell_json}")"
    cell_dir="$(jq -r '.cell_dir' <<<"${cell_json}")"
    pid="$(jq -r '.pid' <<<"${cell_json}")"

    # Try to kill the cell's process group
    if [[ -n "${pid}" ]] && (( pid > 0 )); then
      kill -TERM -"${pid}" >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
    fi

    # Try to docker stop any harbor-runner containers in this cell
    if [[ -d "${cell_dir}" ]]; then
      # The container ID is typically embedded in result.json or we can search by cell_dir
      # For now, we attempt a generic docker stop on containers that might be for this cell
      docker ps -q --filter "label=fizeau-cell=${cell_id}" 2>/dev/null \
        | xargs -r docker stop >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
    fi
  done <<<"${cells}"
}

usage() {
  cat >&2 <<EOF
Usage:
  ./benchmark profiles
  ./benchmark bench-sets
  ./benchmark harness-adapters
  ./benchmark task-executors
  ./benchmark validate
  ./benchmark preflight
  ./benchmark --profile P[,P2,...] --bench-set B[,B2,...] [--plan] [--out DIR]
              [--force-rerun] [--retry-invalid] [--reps N] [--max-cost-usd X]
              [--jobs N]

Flags (run mode):
  --profile P[,P2,...]   Comma-separated profile IDs (required)
  --bench-set B[,B2,...] Comma-separated bench-set IDs (required)
  --plan                 Validate + print matrix; pure, no side effects
  --out DIR              Output root (default: ${DEFAULT_OUT_ROOT}); ignored under --plan
  --force-rerun          Always mint reps fresh cells; ignore terminal report.json
  --retry-invalid        Re-run cells whose final_status is invalid (non-empty
                         invalid_class) or whose cell-state.json is orphan
                         (mid-run crash). New cell links via attempt_of; the
                         prior cell is back-written with superseded_by.
  --reps N               Override bench-set default_reps for every cell
  --max-cost-usd X       USD cap on cumulative cost_usd_at_run_time. When the
                         accumulator reaches the cap, the runner halts before
                         the next cell; remaining cells are written as
                         placeholders with final_status=budget_halted,
                         process_outcome=setup_failed, and a note.
  --jobs N               Maximum concurrent background jobs (default: 1)
EOF
}

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Listing subcommands
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

cmd_profiles() {
  local f
  local entries=()
  shopt -s nullglob
  for f in "${PROFILES_DIR}"/*.yaml "${PROFILES_DIR}"/*.yml; do
    entries+=("$(basename "${f%.*}")")
  done
  shopt -u nullglob
  if [[ ${#entries[@]} -eq 0 ]]; then
    warn "no profiles found in ${PROFILES_DIR}"
    return 0
  fi
  printf '%s\n' "${entries[@]}" | LC_ALL=C sort -u
}

cmd_bench_sets() {
  local f
  local entries=()
  shopt -s nullglob
  for f in "${BENCH_SETS_DIR}"/*.yaml "${BENCH_SETS_DIR}"/*.yml; do
    entries+=("$(basename "${f%.*}")")
  done
  shopt -u nullglob
  if [[ ${#entries[@]} -eq 0 ]]; then
    warn "no bench-sets found in ${BENCH_SETS_DIR}"
    return 0
  fi

# Benchmark Task

You are solving an automated benchmark task. Complete the task fully.

## Rules

- **Verify output**: Before reporting done, confirm required output files exist on disk. Do not declare success without checking.
- **Run the test**: If a test script is present (e.g. `test_outputs.py`, `test.sh`, `run_tests`, `/autograder/run_tests`), run it as your final step.
- **Change on failure**: If a command or approach fails, diagnose and try something different. Never repeat the same failing action.
- **No questions**: Never ask for clarification. Make reasonable assumptions and proceed.

# list_adapter_dir prints one "<name>\t<summary>" line per executable file.
# Files without a `# SUMMARY:` line on line 2 print "(no summary)" so the
# omission is visible. CONTRACT.md and other non-executables are skipped.
list_adapter_dir() {
  local dir="$1"
  [[ -d "${dir}" ]] || { warn "directory missing: ${dir}"; return 0; }
  local f name summary
  local rows=()
  shopt -s nullglob dotglob
  for f in "${dir}"/*; do
    [[ -f "${f}" && -x "${f}" ]] || continue
    name="$(basename "${f}")"
    summary="$(sed -n '2{/^# SUMMARY:[[:space:]]*/{s///;p;};q;}' "${f}")"
    [[ -n "${summary}" ]] || summary="(no summary)"
    rows+=("$(printf '%s\t%s' "${name}" "${summary}")")
  done
  shopt -u nullglob dotglob
  if [[ ${#rows[@]} -eq 0 ]]; then
    warn "no adapters found in ${dir}"
    return 0
  fi
  printf '%s\n' "${rows[@]}" | LC_ALL=C sort -u
}

cmd_harness_adapters() { list_adapter_dir "${HARNESS_ADAPTERS_DIR}"; }
cmd_task_executors() { list_adapter_dir "${TASK_EXECUTORS_DIR}"; }

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Matrix expansion (yq -o=json + jq)
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

resolve_profile_file() {
  local id="$1" f
  for f in "${PROFILES_DIR}/${id}.yaml" "${PROFILES_DIR}/${id}.yml"; do
    if [[ -f "${f}" ]]; then printf '%s\n' "${f}"; return 0; fi
  done
  die "profile not found: ${id} (looked in ${PROFILES_DIR})"
}

resolve_bench_set_file() {
  local id="$1" f
  for f in "${BENCH_SETS_DIR}/${id}.yaml" "${BENCH_SETS_DIR}/${id}.yml"; do
    if [[ -f "${f}" ]]; then printf '%s\n' "${f}"; return 0; fi
  done
  die "bench-set not found: ${id} (looked in ${BENCH_SETS_DIR})"
}

# bench_set_json emits the bench-set as JSON.
bench_set_json() {
  local file="$1"
  yq -o=json '.' "${file}"
}

# resolve_tasks emits one task id per line for a bench-set.
# Handles three shapes:
#   tasks: [id, id, ...]         inline list
#   tasks: all                   defer to harness dataset; emit "*all*"
#   tasks_from: <path>           load tasks[].id from a sibling YAML
resolve_tasks() {
  local bs_file="$1"
  local bs_json tasks_kind tasks_from
  bs_json="$(bench_set_json "${bs_file}")"

  tasks_kind="$(jq -r '
    if (.tasks // null) == null then "none"
    elif (.tasks | type) == "array" then "list"
    elif (.tasks | type) == "string" then ("string:" + .tasks)
    else "other"
    end' <<<"${bs_json}")"

  tasks_from="$(jq -r '.tasks_from // ""' <<<"${bs_json}")"

  if [[ "${tasks_kind}" == "list" ]]; then
    jq -r '.tasks[]' <<<"${bs_json}"
    return 0
  fi
  if [[ "${tasks_kind}" == "string:all" ]]; then
    # Defer to the harness dataset. The plan path emits a sentinel so
    # operators see the contract; the run path (PR A4) expands at execution.
    printf '*all*\n'
    return 0
  fi
  if [[ -n "${tasks_from}" ]]; then
    local resolved
    resolved="$(cd "$(dirname "${bs_file}")" && cd "$(dirname "${tasks_from}")" && pwd)/$(basename "${tasks_from}")"
    [[ -f "${resolved}" ]] || die "tasks_from path not found: ${tasks_from} (resolved to ${resolved})"
    yq -o=json '.' "${resolved}" \
      | jq -r '
          if (.tasks | type) == "array" then
            .tasks[] | (if type == "object" then .id else . end)
          else
            empty
          end'
    return 0
  fi
  die "bench-set ${bs_file}: no .tasks list, .tasks: all, or .tasks_from set"
}

# bench_set_framework prints the framework name for the bench-set.
bench_set_framework() {
  local bs_json
  bs_json="$(bench_set_json "$1")"
  jq -r '.framework // "unknown"' <<<"${bs_json}"
}

# bench_set_dataset prints the dataset name for the bench-set.
bench_set_dataset() {
  local bs_json
  bs_json="$(bench_set_json "$1")"
  jq -r '.dataset // "unknown"' <<<"${bs_json}"
}

# bench_set_reps prints the resolved rep count (default_reps, fallback 1).
bench_set_reps() {
  local bs_json
  bs_json="$(bench_set_json "$1")"
  jq -r '.default_reps // 1' <<<"${bs_json}"
}

# default_task_executor: harbor when terminal-bench, else "unknown".
default_task_executor_for_framework() {
  case "$1" in
    terminal-bench) printf 'harbor\n' ;;
    *)              printf 'unknown\n' ;;
  esac
}

# concurrency_groups_json emits the concurrency-group manifest as JSON.
concurrency_groups_json() {
  local file="${SCRIPT_DIR}/concurrency-groups.yaml"
  [[ -f "${file}" ]] || die "validate: concurrency-groups.yaml missing (${file})"
  yq -o=json '.' "${file}"
}

# build_task_allowlist_file writes a newline-separated allowlist of canonical
# task ids discovered from the checked-in task-subset manifests.
build_task_allowlist_file() {
  local out="$1"
  local subset_files=()
  local f
  shopt -s nullglob
  for f in "${SCRIPT_DIR}"/task-subset-*.yaml; do
    subset_files+=("${f}")
  done
  shopt -u nullglob
  [[ ${#subset_files[@]} -gt 0 ]] || die "validate: no task-subset YAML files found in ${SCRIPT_DIR}"

  : >"${out}"
  for f in "${subset_files[@]}"; do
    yq -o=json '.' "${f}" \
      | jq -r '.tasks[]? | (if type == "object" then (.id // empty) else . end)' \
      >>"${out}"
  done
  LC_ALL=C sort -u "${out}" -o "${out}"
}

# validate_profile_catalog checks that every profile parses, keeps its
# filename/id in sync, references a known concurrency group, and resolves to a
# known harness adapter.
validate_profile_catalog() {
  local concurrency_json="$1"
  local f json name profile_id concurrency_group harness adapter
  local files=()
  shopt -s nullglob
  for f in "${PROFILES_DIR}"/*.yaml "${PROFILES_DIR}"/*.yml; do
    files+=("${f}")
  done
  shopt -u nullglob
  [[ ${#files[@]} -gt 0 ]] || die "validate: no profiles found in ${PROFILES_DIR}"

  for f in "${files[@]}"; do
    name="$(basename "${f%.*}")"
    json="$(yq -o=json '.' "${f}")"
    profile_id="$(jq -r '.id // ""' <<<"${json}")"
    [[ -n "${profile_id}" ]] || die "validate: profile ${name} is missing .id"
    [[ "${profile_id}" == "${name}" ]] || die "validate: profile ${name} id mismatch (${profile_id})"

    concurrency_group="$(jq -r '.concurrency_group // ""' <<<"${json}")"
    [[ -n "${concurrency_group}" ]] || die "validate: profile ${name} is missing .concurrency_group"
    jq -e --arg group "${concurrency_group}" '.groups[$group] != null' <<<"${concurrency_json}" >/dev/null \
      || die "validate: profile ${name} references unknown concurrency_group ${concurrency_group}"

    harness="$(jq -r '.harness // "none"' <<<"${json}")"
    adapter="$(resolve_adapter_for_harness "${harness}")"
    [[ -x "${HARNESS_ADAPTERS_DIR}/${adapter}" ]] \
      || die "validate: profile ${name} harness ${harness} resolves to missing adapter ${adapter}"
  done
}

# validate_bench_sets checks that every bench-set parses, keeps its id synced
# with the filename, and only references known task ids.
validate_bench_sets() {
  local allowlist_file="$1"
  local f json name bench_set_id framework dataset reps task seen_task
  local files=()
  shopt -s nullglob
  for f in "${BENCH_SETS_DIR}"/*.yaml "${BENCH_SETS_DIR}"/*.yml; do
    files+=("${f}")
  done
  shopt -u nullglob
  [[ ${#files[@]} -gt 0 ]] || die "validate: no bench-sets found in ${BENCH_SETS_DIR}"

  for f in "${files[@]}"; do
    name="$(basename "${f%.*}")"
    json="$(bench_set_json "${f}")"
    bench_set_id="$(jq -r '.id // ""' <<<"${json}")"
    [[ -n "${bench_set_id}" ]] || die "validate: bench-set ${name} is missing .id"
    [[ "${bench_set_id}" == "${name}" ]] || die "validate: bench-set ${name} id mismatch (${bench_set_id})"

    framework="$(jq -r '.framework // ""' <<<"${json}")"
    [[ -n "${framework}" ]] || die "validate: bench-set ${name} is missing .framework"
    dataset="$(jq -r '.dataset // ""' <<<"${json}")"
    [[ -n "${dataset}" ]] || die "validate: bench-set ${name} is missing .dataset"
    reps="$(jq -r '.default_reps // 1' <<<"${json}")"
    if ! [[ "${reps}" =~ ^[0-9]+$ ]] || (( reps < 1 )); then
      die "validate: bench-set ${name} default_reps must be a positive integer (got ${reps})"
    fi

    seen_task=0
    while IFS= read -r task; do
      [[ -n "${task}" ]] || continue
      seen_task=1
      [[ "${task}" != "*all*" ]] || continue
      grep -Fxq -- "${task}" "${allowlist_file}" \
        || die "validate: bench-set ${name} references unknown task ${task}"
    done < <(resolve_tasks "${f}")
    [[ ${seen_task} -eq 1 ]] || die "validate: bench-set ${name} resolved no tasks"
  done
}

# validate_harness_adapters checks adapter install/command JSON contracts for
# every executable in scripts/benchmark/harness-adapters/.
validate_harness_adapters() {
  local tmpdir="$1" synthetic_profile="$2"
  local artifact="${tmpdir}/agent-artifact"
  : >"${artifact}"

  local f name install_out command_out install_err command_err install_status command_status
  local files=()
  shopt -s nullglob dotglob
  for f in "${HARNESS_ADAPTERS_DIR}"/*; do
    [[ -f "${f}" && -x "${f}" ]] || continue
    files+=("${f}")
  done
  shopt -u nullglob dotglob
  [[ ${#files[@]} -gt 0 ]] || die "validate: no harness adapters found in ${HARNESS_ADAPTERS_DIR}"

  for f in "${files[@]}"; do
    name="$(basename "${f}")"
    install_err="${tmpdir}/${name}.install.err"
    command_err="${tmpdir}/${name}.command.err"

    set +e
    install_out="$("${f}" install "${artifact}" 2>"${install_err}")"
    install_status=$?
    set -e
    if [[ ${install_status} -ne 0 ]]; then
      die "validate: harness-adapter ${name} install failed: $(<"${install_err}")"
    fi
    jq -e '
      type == "object"
      and (.install_command | type == "string" and length > 0)
      and (.artifact_source | type == "string" and length > 0)
      and (.binary_path | type == "string")
      and (has("harbor_plugin"))
    ' <<<"${install_out}" >/dev/null \
      || die "validate: harness-adapter ${name} install output is malformed"

    set +e
    command_out="$(printf '%s' "${synthetic_profile}" | "${f}" command 2>"${command_err}")"
    command_status=$?
    set -e
    if [[ ${command_status} -ne 0 ]]; then
      die "validate: harness-adapter ${name} command failed: $(<"${command_err}")"
    fi
    jq -e '
      type == "object"

bp="$(budget_path "${out}")"
  if [[ -f "${bp}" ]]; then
    local prev_cap
    prev_cap="$(jq -r '.max_cost_usd // ""' "${bp}" 2>/dev/null || true)"
    if [[ "${prev_cap}" == "${cap}" ]]; then
      return 0
    fi
  fi
  jq -n \
    --argjson cap "${cap}" \
    '{max_cost_usd:$cap, total_cost_usd:0, halted:false, cells:[]}' \
    >"${bp}.tmp"
  mv "${bp}.tmp" "${bp}"
}

# append_budget_cell folds one closed cell's cost into budget.json and prints
# the post-update total_cost_usd. Halted flag flips true when the running
# total meets or exceeds the cap.
append_budget_cell() {
  local out="$1" cell_id="$2" task="$3" profile_id="$4" cost="$5"
  local bp tmp
  bp="$(budget_path "${out}")"
  tmp="$(mktemp)"
  jq \
    --arg cell_id "${cell_id}" \
    --arg task "${task}" \
    --arg profile "${profile_id}" \
    --argjson cost "${cost}" \
    '
      .cells += [{cell_id:$cell_id, task:$task, profile:$profile, cost_usd:$cost}]
      | .total_cost_usd = ((.total_cost_usd // 0) + $cost)
      | .halted = ((.total_cost_usd >= (.max_cost_usd // 0))
                   and ((.max_cost_usd // 0) > 0))
    ' "${bp}" >"${tmp}"
  mv "${tmp}" "${bp}"
  jq -r '.total_cost_usd' "${bp}"
}

# budget_is_halted exits 0 when the budget cap has been hit (halted=true in
# budget.json). Used by run_sweep to gate cells before invoking the executor.
budget_is_halted() {
  local bp
  bp="$(budget_path "$1")"
  [[ -f "${bp}" ]] || return 1
  local halted
  halted="$(jq -r '.halted // false' "${bp}")"
  [[ "${halted}" == "true" ]]
}

# write_budget_halted_cell writes a placeholder cell that did not execute
# because the budget cap had already been hit. final_status=budget_halted,
# process_outcome=setup_failed, plus a 'note' that quotes the cap.
write_budget_halted_cell() {
  local out="$1" profile_id="$2" profile_file="$3"
  local framework="$4" dataset="$5" dataset_dir="$6" task="$7"
  local rep_idx="$8" total_reps="$9" cap="${10}"
  local te_ver="${11}" hd="${12}"

  local cell_id cell_dir
  cell_id="$(new_cell_id)"
  cell_dir="${out}/cells/${dataset_dir}/${task}/${cell_id}"
  mkdir -p "${cell_dir}/session"
  : >"${cell_dir}/fiz.txt"
  : >"${cell_dir}/fiz.err"

  local profile_json bp total
  profile_json="$(yq -o=json '.' "${profile_file}")"
  bp="$(budget_path "${out}")"
  total="$(jq -r '.total_cost_usd // 0' "${bp}" 2>/dev/null || printf '0')"

  local now rep_str note
  now="$(date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ)"
  rep_str="$(printf '%d/%d' "${rep_idx}" "${total_reps}")"
  note="$(printf 'halted before cell: --max-cost-usd=%s reached (cumulative cost_usd_at_run_time=%s)' \
                  "${cap}" "${total}")"

  jq -n \
    --argjson profile "${profile_json}" \
    --arg task_id "${task}" \
    --arg framework "${framework}" \
    --arg dataset "${dataset}" \
    --arg cell_id "${cell_id}" \
    --arg now "${now}" \
    --arg rep "${rep_str}" \
    --arg te_ver "${te_ver}" \
    --arg hd "${hd}" \
    --arg note "${note}" \
    --argjson cost 0 \
    '{
       task_id:$task_id,
       framework:$framework,
       dataset:$dataset,
       cell_id:$cell_id,
       started_at:$now,
       finished_at:$now,
       rep:$rep,
       profile:$profile,
       command:[],
       env_redacted:{},
       artifacts:{fiz_txt:"fiz.txt", fiz_err:"fiz.err", session_dir:"session"},
       result:{},
       final_status:"budget_halted",
       invalid_class:"",
       process_outcome:"setup_failed",
       note:$note,
       cost_usd_at_run_time:$cost,
       task_executor_version:$te_ver,
       harbor_runner_image_digest:$hd
     }' >"${cell_dir}/report.json.tmp"
  mv "${cell_dir}/report.json.tmp" "${cell_dir}/report.json"
  printf '%s\n' "${cell_dir}"
}

# Run one cell start-to-finish: cell-state sentinel, harness-adapter command,
# task-spec composition, task-executor invocation with retry, report.json
# composition, sentinel cleanup, optional back-write of superseded_by into
# the prior (attempt_of) cell. Registers as in-flight at start, unregisters at end.
run_one_cell() {
  local out="$1" profile_id="$2" profile_file="$3"
  local framework="$4" dataset="$5" dataset_dir="$6" task="$7"
  local rep_idx="$8" total_reps="$9" harness_adapter="${10}"
  local attempt_of="${11}"
  local te_ver="${12}" hd="${13}"
  local interrupted_flag="${14:-}"

  local cell_id cell_dir
  cell_id="$(new_cell_id)"
  cell_dir="${out}/cells/${dataset_dir}/${task}/${cell_id}"
  mkdir -p "${cell_dir}"

  # Handler for SIGTERM during cell execution
  write_interrupted_report() {
    local now
    now="$(date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ)"
    jq -n \
      --arg cell_id "${cell_id}" \
      --arg task "${task}" \
      --arg profile "${profile_id}" \
      --arg now "${now}" \
      --arg te_ver "${te_ver}" \
      --arg hd "${hd}" \
      '{
         cell_id:$cell_id,
         task_id:$task,
         framework:"terminal-bench",
         dataset:"terminal-bench-2-1",
         started_at:$now,
         finished_at:$now,
         rep:"1/1",
         final_status:"interrupted",
         invalid_class:"",
         process_outcome:"killed",
         cost_usd_at_run_time:0,
         task_executor_version:$te_ver,
         harbor_runner_image_digest:$hd
       }' >"${cell_dir}/report.json.tmp"
    mv "${cell_dir}/report.json.tmp" "${cell_dir}/report.json"
  }

  # Register as in-flight so signal handlers can interrupt us
  register_inflight "${cell_id}" "${cell_dir}"
  trap 'write_interrupted_report; unregister_inflight "${cell_id}"; trap - RETURN TERM; exit 143' TERM
  trap 'unregister_inflight "${cell_id}"; trap - RETURN TERM' RETURN

  # Check if global interrupt flag is set before starting
  if [[ -n "${interrupted_flag}" && -f "${interrupted_flag}" ]]; then
    warn "cell ${cell_id}: halting due to signal interrupt"
    # Write interrupted report immediately
    write_interrupted_report
    printf '%s\n' "${cell_dir}"
    return 0
  fi

  local started_at
  started_at="$(date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ)"
  jq -n \
    --arg cell_id "${cell_id}" \
    --arg task "${task}" \
    --arg profile "${profile_id}" \
    --arg started "${started_at}" \
    '{cell_id:$cell_id, task:$task, profile:$profile, started_at:$started, status:"running"}' \
    >"${cell_dir}/cell-state.json"

  local profile_json
  profile_json="$(yq -o=json '.' "${profile_file}")"

  local adapter_path="${HARNESS_ADAPTERS_DIR}/${harness_adapter}"
  [[ -x "${adapter_path}" ]] || die "harness-adapter missing/not executable: ${adapter_path}"

  local command_spec install_spec harbor_plugin
  install_spec="$("${adapter_path}" install "/installed-agent/${harness_adapter}")" \
    || die "harness-adapter ${harness_adapter} install failed"
  harbor_plugin="$(jq -r '.harbor_plugin // ""' <<<"${install_spec}")"
  command_spec="$(printf '%s' "${profile_json}" | "${adapter_path}" command)" \
    || die "harness-adapter ${harness_adapter} command failed"

  local env_json secret_keys_json command_json env_redacted
  env_json="$(jq -c '.env // {}' <<<"${command_spec}")"
  secret_keys_json="$(jq -c '.secret_env_keys // []' <<<"${command_spec}")"
  command_json="$(jq -c '.command // []' <<<"${command_spec}")"
  env_redacted="$(compose_env_redacted "${env_json}" "${secret_keys_json}")"

  local tasks_dir
  tasks_dir="$(resolve_tasks_dir "${framework}")"

  local task_spec
  task_spec="$(jq -nc \
    --arg task_id "${task}" \
    --arg tasks_dir "${tasks_dir}" \
    --arg cell_dir "${cell_dir}" \
    --arg plugin "${harbor_plugin}" \
    --arg image "${HARBOR_IMAGE_TAG}" \
    --argjson env "${env_json}" \
    --argjson secret_env_keys "${secret_keys_json}" \
    '{task_id:$task_id, tasks_dir:$tasks_dir, cell_dir:$cell_dir,
      harbor_plugin:$plugin, image:$image, env:$env,
      secret_env_keys:$secret_env_keys, extra_args:[]}')"

  local executor="${TASK_EXECUTORS_DIR}/harbor"
  if [[ -n "${BENCH_TASK_EXECUTOR_OVERRIDE:-}" ]]; then
    executor="${BENCH_TASK_EXECUTOR_OVERRIDE}"
  fi
  [[ -x "${executor}" ]] || die "task-executor missing/not executable: ${executor}"

  local exec_status=0
  run_executor_with_retry "${task_spec}" "${cell_dir}" "${executor}" || exec_status=$?

  local finished_at
  finished_at="$(date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ)"

  local result_json="{}"
  if [[ -s "${cell_dir}/result.json" ]]; then
    if jq -e '.' "${cell_dir}/result.json" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
      result_json="$(cat "${cell_dir}/result.json")"
    fi
  fi

  local final_status invalid_class
  if [[ ${exec_status} -eq 0 ]]; then
    final_status="$(jq -r '.final_status // .status // "completed"' <<<"${result_json}")"
    invalid_class="$(jq -r '.invalid_class // ""' <<<"${result_json}")"
  else
    final_status="transient_exhausted"
    invalid_class="$(jq -r '.invalid_class // "transient_exhausted"' <<<"${result_json}")"
  fi

  [[ -f "${cell_dir}/fiz.txt" ]] || : >"${cell_dir}/fiz.txt"
  [[ -f "${cell_dir}/fiz.err" ]] || : >"${cell_dir}/fiz.err"
  mkdir -p "${cell_dir}/session"

  local rep_str
  rep_str="$(printf '%d/%d' "${rep_idx}" "${total_reps}")"

  local cost_usd
  cost_usd="$(compute_cell_cost "${result_json}" "${profile_json}")"
  [[ -n "${cost_usd}" ]] || cost_usd="0"

  local report
  report="$(jq -n \
    --argjson profile "${profile_json}" \
    --argjson command "${command_json}" \
    --argjson env_redacted "${env_redacted}" \
    --argjson result "${result_json}" \
    --arg task_id "${task}" \
    --arg framework "${framework}" \
    --arg dataset "${dataset}" \
    --arg cell_id "${cell_id}" \
    --arg started_at "${started_at}" \
    --arg finished_at "${finished_at}" \
    --arg final_status "${final_status}" \
    --arg invalid_class "${invalid_class}" \
    --arg te_ver "${te_ver}" \
    --arg hd "${hd}" \
    --arg rep "${rep_str}" \
    --arg attempt_of "${attempt_of}" \
    --argjson cost "${cost_usd}" \
    '{
       task_id:$task_id,
       framework:$framework,
       dataset:$dataset,
       cell_id:$cell_id,
       started_at:$started_at,
       finished_at:$finished_at,
       rep:$rep,
       profile:$profile,
       command:$command,
       env_redacted:$env_redacted,
       artifacts:{fiz_txt:"fiz.txt", fiz_err:"fiz.err", session_dir:"session"},
       result:$result,
       final_status:$final_status,
       invalid_class:$invalid_class,
       cost_usd_at_run_time:$cost,
       task_executor_version:$te_ver,
       harbor_runner_image_digest:$hd
     } | if $attempt_of != "" then . + {attempt_of:$attempt_of} else . end')"

  printf '%s\n' "${report}" >"${cell_dir}/report.json.tmp"
  mv "${cell_dir}/report.json.tmp" "${cell_dir}/report.json"

  rm -f "${cell_dir}/cell-state.json"

  if [[ -n "${attempt_of}" && -f "${attempt_of}/report.json" ]]; then
    local tmp
    tmp="$(mktemp)"
    jq --arg sup "${cell_id}" '. + {superseded_by:$sup}' \
      "${attempt_of}/report.json" >"${tmp}"
    mv "${tmp}" "${attempt_of}/report.json"
  elif [[ -n "${attempt_of}" ]]; then
    # Orphan prior cell with no report.json: write a minimal stub so the
    # superseded_by back-link is recorded.
    jq -n \
      --arg sup "${cell_id}" \
      --arg orphan_of "$(basename "${attempt_of}")" \
      '{final_status:"orphan", invalid_class:"orphan_cell_state", cell_id:$orphan_of, superseded_by:$sup}' \
      >"${attempt_of}/report.json"
    rm -f "${attempt_of}/cell-state.json"
  fi

  # Unregister from in-flight (trap will also call this on return)
  unregister_inflight "${cell_id}"
  printf '%s\n' "${cell_dir}"
}

# budget_or_run_cell either writes a budget-halted placeholder (when the
# budget cap has been hit) or invokes run_one_cell. On success it appends the
# cell's cost into <out>/budget.json so the halt flag flips for the next
# cell. cap="" disables budget machinery entirely. interrupted_flag="" disables
# interrupt checking.
budget_or_run_cell() {
  local out="$1" profile_id="$2" profile_file="$3"
  local framework="$4" dataset="$5" dataset_dir="$6" task="$7"
  local rep_idx="$8" total_reps="$9" adapter="${10}" attempt_of="${11}"
  local te_ver="${12}" hd="${13}" cap="${14}" interrupted_flag="${15:-}"

  local cell_dir
  if [[ -n "${cap}" ]] && budget_is_halted "${out}"; then
    cell_dir="$(write_budget_halted_cell "${out}" "${profile_id}" "${profile_file}" \
                  "${framework}" "${dataset}" "${dataset_dir}" "${task}" \
                  "${rep_idx}" "${total_reps}" "${cap}" "${te_ver}" "${hd}")"
    printf '%s\n' "${cell_dir}"
    return 0
  fi

  cell_dir="$(run_one_cell "${out}" "${profile_id}" "${profile_file}" \
                "${framework}" "${dataset}" "${dataset_dir}" "${task}" \
                "${rep_idx}" "${total_reps}" "${adapter}" "${attempt_of}" \
                "${te_ver}" "${hd}" "${interrupted_flag}")"

  if [[ -n "${cap}" && -f "${cell_dir}/report.json" ]]; then
    local cell_id cost
    cell_id="$(jq -r '.cell_id // ""' "${cell_dir}/report.json")"
    cost="$(jq -r '.cost_usd_at_run_time // 0' "${cell_dir}/report.json")"
    [[ -n "${cost}" ]] || cost=0
    append_budget_cell "${out}" "${cell_id}" "${task}" "${profile_id}" "${cost}" >/dev/null
  fi
  printf '%s\n' "${cell_dir}"
}

# run_sweep walks the (profile, bench-set, task, rep) matrix, applies resume /
# retry rules, and runs one cell per slot. Supports concurrency-group locking,
# background job pool (--jobs), and signal handling (SIGINT/SIGTERM).
run_sweep() {
  local profiles_csv="$1" bench_sets_csv="$2" out="$3"
  local force_rerun="$4" retry_invalid="$5" reps_override="$6"
  local max_cost_usd="$7" jobs="${8:-1}"

  require_tool yq
  require_tool jq
  require_tool sha256sum

  mkdir -p "${out}" "${FIZEAU_BENCH_STATE_DIR}"

  local te_ver hd
  te_ver="$(task_executor_version)"
  hd="$(harbor_runner_image_digest)"
  write_sweep_metadata "${out}" "${te_ver}" "${hd}"

  if [[ -n "${max_cost_usd}" ]]; then
    init_budget "${out}" "${max_cost_usd}"
  fi

  # Signal handling: set up interrupt flag and trap
  local interrupted_flag
  interrupted_flag="${FIZEAU_BENCH_STATE_DIR}/.benchmark-interrupt-$$"

  # Trap handler for SIGINT/SIGTERM: interrupt running cells, wait for cleanup
  handle_interrupt() {
    local grace_seconds="${BENCH_TERM_GRACE_SECONDS}"
    [[ -f "${interrupted_flag}" ]] && return 0
    warn "Received signal, stopping cell submission and interrupting running cells..."
    touch "${interrupted_flag}"

    # Terminate in-flight cells
    interrupt_inflight_cells

    # Send SIGTERM to all child processes (the background job pool)
    local pids
    pids="$(jobs -p)"
    if [[ -n "${pids}" ]]; then
      kill -TERM ${pids} 2>/dev/null || true

# Wait up to grace_seconds for them to exit
      local elapsed=0
      while (( elapsed < grace_seconds )); do
        pids="$(jobs -p)"
        if [[ -z "${pids}" ]]; then
          break
        fi
        sleep 1
        elapsed=$((elapsed + 1))
      done

      # Force kill any stragglers
      pids="$(jobs -p)"
      if [[ -n "${pids}" ]]; then
        kill -KILL ${pids} 2>/dev/null || true
      fi
    fi
  }

  trap 'handle_interrupt' SIGINT SIGTERM

  local active_jobs=0

  IFS=',' read -r -a profile_ids <<<"${profiles_csv}"
  IFS=',' read -r -a bench_set_ids <<<"${bench_sets_csv}"

  local profile_id bs_id bs_file profile_file
  local framework dataset dataset_dir reps tasks harness adapter
  local terminal_file invalid_file orphan_file
  local concurrency_group

  for profile_id in "${profile_ids[@]}"; do
    [[ -n "${profile_id}" ]] || continue
    profile_file="$(resolve_profile_file "${profile_id}")"
    local profile_json_full harness
    profile_json_full="$(yq -o=json '.' "${profile_file}")"
    harness="$(jq -r '.harness // "none"' <<<"${profile_json_full}")"
    adapter="$(resolve_adapter_for_harness "${harness}")"
    concurrency_group="$(jq -r '.concurrency_group // "default"' <<<"${profile_json_full}")"

    for bs_id in "${bench_set_ids[@]}"; do
      [[ -n "${bs_id}" ]] || continue
      bs_file="$(resolve_bench_set_file "${bs_id}")"
      framework="$(bench_set_framework "${bs_file}")"
      dataset="$(bench_set_dataset "${bs_file}")"
      dataset_dir="${dataset}"
      if [[ -n "${reps_override}" ]]; then
        reps="${reps_override}"
      else
        reps="$(bench_set_reps "${bs_file}")"
      fi
      tasks="$(resolve_tasks "${bs_file}")"

      local task
      while IFS= read -r task; do
        [[ -n "${task}" ]] || continue
        [[ "${task}" != "*all*" ]] || die "bench-set ${bs_id}: tasks: all is not supported by run path yet"

        terminal_file="$(mktemp)"; invalid_file="$(mktemp)"; orphan_file="$(mktemp)"
        inspect_existing_cells "${out}" "${dataset_dir}" "${task}" "${profile_id}" \
          "${terminal_file}" "${invalid_file}" "${orphan_file}"

        local terminal_count invalid_count orphan_count
        terminal_count="$(wc -l <"${terminal_file}" | tr -d ' ')"
        invalid_count="$(wc -l <"${invalid_file}" | tr -d ' ')"
        orphan_count="$(wc -l <"${orphan_file}" | tr -d ' ')"

        local cells_to_run=()
        if [[ ${force_rerun} -eq 1 ]]; then
          local r
          for ((r = 1; r <= reps; r++)); do
            cells_to_run+=("${profile_id}|${profile_file}|${framework}|${dataset}|${dataset_dir}|${task}|${r}|${reps}|${adapter}||${te_ver}|${hd}|${max_cost_usd}|${concurrency_group}")
          done
        elif [[ ${retry_invalid} -eq 1 ]]; then
          local prior
          while IFS= read -r prior; do
            [[ -n "${prior}" ]] || continue
            cells_to_run+=("${profile_id}|${profile_file}|${framework}|${dataset}|${dataset_dir}|${task}|1|${reps}|${adapter}|${prior}|${te_ver}|${hd}|${max_cost_usd}|${concurrency_group}")
          done <"${invalid_file}"
          while IFS= read -r prior; do
            [[ -n "${prior}" ]] || continue
            cells_to_run+=("${profile_id}|${profile_file}|${framework}|${dataset}|${dataset_dir}|${task}|1|${reps}|${adapter}|${prior}|${te_ver}|${hd}|${max_cost_usd}|${concurrency_group}")
          done <"${orphan_file}"
          warn "retry-invalid: profile=${profile_id} task=${task} reran invalid=${invalid_count} orphan=${orphan_count}"
        else
          if [[ ${terminal_count} -ge ${reps} ]]; then
            warn "skip: profile=${profile_id} task=${task} (terminal_cells=${terminal_count} >= reps=${reps})"
          else
            local need r idx
            need=$((reps - terminal_count))
            idx=$((terminal_count + 1))
            for ((r = 1; r <= need; r++)); do
              cells_to_run+=("${profile_id}|${profile_file}|${framework}|${dataset}|${dataset_dir}|${task}|${idx}|${reps}|${adapter}||${te_ver}|${hd}|${max_cost_usd}|${concurrency_group}")
              idx=$((idx + 1))
            done
          fi
        fi

        # Run cells with job pool and concurrency-group locking
        for cell_spec in "${cells_to_run[@]}"; do
          # Wait for a job slot to become available
          while (( active_jobs >= jobs )); do
            wait -n 2>/dev/null || true
            active_jobs=$((active_jobs - 1))
          done

          # Check if we've been interrupted
          if [[ -f "${interrupted_flag}" ]]; then
            warn "Stopping cell submission due to signal interrupt"
            # Wait for remaining jobs
            wait
            rm -f "${interrupted_flag}" "${terminal_file}" "${invalid_file}" "${orphan_file}"
            trap - SIGINT SIGTERM
            return 130
          fi

          # Parse cell spec
          IFS='|' read -r p_id p_file fw ds ds_dir tsk rep_idx t_reps adp att_of te_v hd_v cap cg <<<"${cell_spec}"

          # Acquire concurrency-group lock
          local lock_fd
          lock_fd="$(acquire_group_lock "${cg}")"

          # Run cell in background
          (
            budget_or_run_cell "${out}" "${p_id}" "${p_file}" \
              "${fw}" "${ds}" "${ds_dir}" "${tsk}" \
              "${rep_idx}" "${t_reps}" "${adp}" "${att_of}" \
              "${te_v}" "${hd_v}" "${cap}" "${interrupted_flag}"
            release_group_lock "${lock_fd}"
          ) &

          active_jobs=$((active_jobs + 1))
        done

        rm -f "${terminal_file}" "${invalid_file}" "${orphan_file}"
      done <<<"${tasks}"
    done
  done

  # Wait for all background jobs to complete
  wait
  local exit_code=0
  [[ -f "${interrupted_flag}" ]] && exit_code=130
  rm -f "${interrupted_flag}"
  trap - SIGINT SIGTERM
  return "${exit_code}"
}

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# validate checks the offline manifest surface without touching the network.
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

cmd_validate() {
  require_tool yq
  require_tool jq

  local tmpdir allowlist_file concurrency_json synthetic_profile synthetic_task_spec
  tmpdir="$(mktemp -d)"
  allowlist_file="${tmpdir}/task-allowlist.txt"

  build_task_allowlist_file "${allowlist_file}"
  concurrency_json="$(concurrency_groups_json)"
  jq -e '
    (.groups // {}) | type == "object"
    and (to_entries | length > 0)
    and all(to_entries[]; (.value.max_concurrency | type == "number") and (.value.max_concurrency > 0))
  ' <<<"${concurrency_json}" >/dev/null \
    || die "validate: concurrency-groups.yaml is malformed"

  validate_profile_catalog "${concurrency_json}"
  validate_bench_sets "${allowlist_file}"

  synthetic_profile="$(jq -nc '{
    id: "validate-profile",
    harness: "none",
    surface: "fiz_provider_native",
    concurrency_group: "openrouter",
    provider: {
      type: "openrouter",
      model: "openai/gpt-5.4-mini",
      base_url: "https://openrouter.ai/api/v1",
      api_key_env: "OPENROUTER_API_KEY"
    },
    sampling: {
      temperature: 0.0,
      reasoning: "low",
      planning_mode: true,
      top_p: 0.95,
      top_k: 20,
      min_p: 0
    },
    limits: {
      max_output_tokens: 128000,
      context_tokens: 1000000
    },
    metadata: {
      runtime: "validate"
    }
  }')"
  validate_harness_adapters "${tmpdir}" "${synthetic_profile}"

  mkdir -p "${tmpdir}/tasks" "${tmpdir}/cell"
  synthetic_task_spec="$(jq -nc --arg tasks_dir "${tmpdir}/tasks" --arg cell_dir "${tmpdir}/cell" '{
    task_id: "validate-task",
    tasks_dir: $tasks_dir,
    cell_dir: $cell_dir,
    harbor_plugin: "scripts.benchmark.harbor_agent:FizeauAgent",
    image: "fizeau-harbor-runner:latest",
    env: {
      FIZEAU_BASE_URL: "https://openrouter.ai/api/v1",
      FIZEAU_MODEL: "openai/gpt-5.4-mini",
      FIZEAU_API_KEY: "${OPENROUTER_API_KEY}"
    },
    secret_env_keys: ["FIZEAU_API_KEY"],
    extra_args: []
  }')"
  validate_task_executors "${tmpdir}" "${synthetic_task_spec}"

  return 0
}

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# main dispatch
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

main() {
  if [[ $# -eq 0 ]]; then
    usage
    exit 2
  fi

  local profile_csv=""
  local bench_set_csv=""
  local plan=0
  local out=""
  local force_rerun=0
  local retry_invalid=0
  local reps_override=""
  local max_cost_usd=""
  local jobs=1
  local positional=()

  while [[ $# -gt 0 ]]; do
    case "$1" in
      --profile)        profile_csv="${2:?--profile requires a value}"; shift 2 ;;
      --bench-set)      bench_set_csv="${2:?--bench-set requires a value}"; shift 2 ;;
      --plan)           plan=1; shift ;;
      --out)            out="${2:?--out requires a value}"; shift 2 ;;
      --force-rerun)    force_rerun=1; shift ;;
      --retry-invalid)  retry_invalid=1; shift ;;
      --reps)           reps_override="${2:?--reps requires a value}"; shift 2 ;;
      --max-cost-usd)   max_cost_usd="${2:?--max-cost-usd requires a value}"; shift 2 ;;
      --jobs)           jobs="${2:?--jobs requires a value}"; shift 2 ;;
      -h|--help|help)   usage; exit 0 ;;
      --)               shift; positional+=("$@"); break ;;
      -*)               die "unknown flag: $1" ;;
      *)                positional+=("$1"); shift ;;
    esac
  done

  local sub=""
  if [[ ${#positional[@]} -gt 0 ]]; then
    sub="${positional[0]}"
  fi

  case "${sub}" in
    profiles)          cmd_profiles ;;
    bench-sets)        cmd_bench_sets ;;
    harness-adapters)  cmd_harness_adapters ;;
    task-executors)    cmd_task_executors ;;
    validate)          cmd_validate ;;
    preflight)         cmd_preflight ;;
    "")
      require_tool yq
      require_tool jq
      [[ -n "${profile_csv}" ]]   || die "--profile is required"
      [[ -n "${bench_set_csv}" ]] || die "--bench-set is required"
      if [[ ${plan} -eq 1 ]]; then
        print_plan "${profile_csv}" "${bench_set_csv}"
        exit 0
      fi
      if [[ -z "${out}" ]]; then
        out="${DEFAULT_OUT_ROOT}"
      fi
      if [[ ${force_rerun} -eq 1 && ${retry_invalid} -eq 1 ]]; then
        die "--force-rerun and --retry-invalid are mutually exclusive"
      fi
      if [[ -n "${max_cost_usd}" ]]; then
        # Validate numeric and non-negative; jq parses with no locale surprises.
        if ! printf '%s' "${max_cost_usd}" \
              | jq -e 'tonumber | . >= 0' >/dev/null 2>&1; then
          die "--max-cost-usd requires a non-negative number (got: ${max_cost_usd})"
        fi
      fi
      run_sweep "${profile_csv}" "${bench_set_csv}" "${out}" \
        "${force_rerun}" "${retry_invalid}" "${reps_override}" \
        "${max_cost_usd}" "${jobs}"
      ;;
    *)
      die "unknown subcommand: ${sub}"
      ;;
  esac
}

main "$@"

and (.command | type == "array" and length > 0)
      and all(.command[]?; type == "string")
      and (.env | type == "object")
      and all((.env // {}) | to_entries[]?; (.value | type == "string"))
      and (.secret_env_keys | type == "array")
      and ((.secret_env_keys | unique | length) == (.secret_env_keys | length))
      and (.env as $env | all(.secret_env_keys[]?; $env[.] != null))
    ' <<<"${command_out}" >/dev/null \
      || die "validate: harness-adapter ${name} command output is malformed"
  done
}

# validate_task_executors checks that every task-executor honors the dry-run
# contract and writes a valid result.json.
validate_task_executors() {
  local tmpdir="$1" synthetic_task_spec="$2"
  local f name stdout_file stderr_file cell_dir result_path status
  local files=()
  shopt -s nullglob dotglob
  for f in "${TASK_EXECUTORS_DIR}"/*; do
    [[ -f "${f}" && -x "${f}" ]] || continue
    files+=("${f}")
  done
  shopt -u nullglob dotglob
  [[ ${#files[@]} -gt 0 ]] || die "validate: no task executors found in ${TASK_EXECUTORS_DIR}"

  for f in "${files[@]}"; do
    name="$(basename "${f}")"
    cell_dir="${tmpdir}/cell"
    mkdir -p "${cell_dir}"
    stdout_file="${tmpdir}/${name}.stdout"
    stderr_file="${tmpdir}/${name}.stderr"
    result_path="${cell_dir}/result.json"

    set +e
    printf '%s' "${synthetic_task_spec}" | HARBOR_TASK_EXECUTOR_DRY_RUN=1 "${f}" \
      >"${stdout_file}" 2>"${stderr_file}"
    status=$?
    set -e
    if [[ ${status} -ne 0 ]]; then
      die "validate: task-executor ${name} failed: $(<"${stderr_file}")"
    fi
    [[ -s "${result_path}" ]] || die "validate: task-executor ${name} did not write ${result_path}"
    jq -e '.' "${result_path}" >/dev/null \
      || die "validate: task-executor ${name} wrote invalid JSON to result.json"
    jq -e '.' "${stdout_file}" >/dev/null \
      || die "validate: task-executor ${name} wrote invalid JSON to stdout"
  done
}

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# --plan: print matrix
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

# print_plan emits one line per (profile, task, rep) cell. Pure: no writes.
print_plan() {
  local profiles_csv="$1" bench_sets_csv="$2"
  IFS=',' read -r -a profile_ids <<<"${profiles_csv}"
  IFS=',' read -r -a bench_set_ids <<<"${bench_sets_csv}"

  local profile_id bs_id bs_file framework dataset reps task_executor tasks
  local rep i n

  for profile_id in "${profile_ids[@]}"; do
    [[ -n "${profile_id}" ]] || continue
    resolve_profile_file "${profile_id}" >/dev/null  # existence check
    for bs_id in "${bench_set_ids[@]}"; do
      [[ -n "${bs_id}" ]] || continue
      bs_file="$(resolve_bench_set_file "${bs_id}")"
      framework="$(bench_set_framework "${bs_file}")"
      dataset="$(bench_set_dataset "${bs_file}")"
      reps="$(bench_set_reps "${bs_file}")"
      task_executor="$(default_task_executor_for_framework "${framework}")"
      tasks="$(resolve_tasks "${bs_file}")"
      i=0
      while IFS= read -r task; do
        [[ -n "${task}" ]] || continue
        i=$((i + 1))
        for ((rep = 1; rep <= reps; rep++)); do
          n=$((rep))
          printf 'profile=%s\tbench_set=%s\tframework=%s\tdataset=%s\ttask=%s\trep=%d/%d\ttask_executor=%s\n' \
            "${profile_id}" "${bs_id}" "${framework}" "${dataset}" \
            "${task}" "${n}" "${reps}" "${task_executor}"
        done
      done <<<"${tasks}"
      [[ "${i}" -gt 0 ]] || die "bench-set ${bs_id}: no tasks resolved"
    done
  done
}

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# preflight: rebuild harbor-runner if image-content-sha drifted
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

# compute_harbor_source_sha mirrors build-harbor-runner.sh's algorithm: a
# sha256 over harbor_adapters/* + harbor_agent.py + Dockerfile.harbor-runner.
# Kept in sync by re-computing the same inputs; the two scripts MUST agree.
compute_harbor_source_sha() {
  local adapters_dir="${SCRIPT_DIR}/harbor_adapters"
  local agent_file="${SCRIPT_DIR}/harbor_agent.py"
  local dockerfile="${SCRIPT_DIR}/Dockerfile.harbor-runner"
  [[ -d "${adapters_dir}" ]] || die "preflight: harbor_adapters/ missing (${adapters_dir})"
  [[ -f "${agent_file}" ]] || die "preflight: harbor_agent.py missing (${agent_file})"
  [[ -f "${dockerfile}" ]] || die "preflight: Dockerfile.harbor-runner missing (${dockerfile})"
  {
    LC_ALL=C find "${adapters_dir}" -type f \
      | LC_ALL=C sort \
      | while IFS= read -r f; do
          printf '%s  %s\n' "$(sha256sum "${f}" | awk '{print $1}')" "${f#"${SCRIPT_DIR}/"}"
        done
    printf '%s  %s\n' "$(sha256sum "${agent_file}" | awk '{print $1}')" "harbor_agent.py"
    printf '%s  %s\n' "$(sha256sum "${dockerfile}" | awk '{print $1}')" "Dockerfile.harbor-runner"
  } | sha256sum | awk '{print $1}'
}

# cached_harbor_image_sha prints the image-content-sha label of the cached
# image, or empty if the image is absent / the label is missing.
cached_harbor_image_sha() {
  local tag="$1"
  command -v docker >/dev/null 2>&1 || { printf ''; return 0; }
  docker image inspect --format '{{ index .Config.Labels "image-content-sha" }}' "${tag}" 2>/dev/null || printf ''
}

cmd_preflight() {
  require_tool sha256sum
  local source_sha cached_sha
  source_sha="$(compute_harbor_source_sha)"
  cached_sha="$(cached_harbor_image_sha "${HARBOR_IMAGE_TAG}")"
  printf 'preflight: source image-content-sha=%s\n' "${source_sha}"
  printf 'preflight: cached image-content-sha=%s\n' "${cached_sha:-<none>}"
  if [[ -n "${cached_sha}" && "${cached_sha}" == "${source_sha}" ]]; then
    printf 'preflight: harbor-runner image up to date; skipping rebuild\n'
    return 0
  fi
  [[ -x "${HARBOR_BUILD_SCRIPT}" ]] || die "preflight: build script missing or not executable: ${HARBOR_BUILD_SCRIPT}"
  require_tool docker
  printf 'preflight: rebuilding harbor-runner (source sha differs)\n'
  IMAGE_TAG="${HARBOR_IMAGE_TAG}" "${HARBOR_BUILD_SCRIPT}"
}

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Run path: per-cell execution loop, resume, retry, sweep metadata
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

# Terminal final_status values that satisfy resume coverage. Non-terminal
# statuses (running, transient_exhausted, missing_result) do not count as
# coverage; --retry-invalid surfaces invalid_class != "" or orphan state.
is_terminal_status() {
  case "$1" in
    completed|pass|fail|timeout|invalid|completed_no_grade) return 0 ;;
    *) return 1 ;;
  esac
}

# Map the profile's `harness:` field to a harness-adapter file name.
# `none` (or absent) means native fiz.
resolve_adapter_for_harness() {
  case "${1:-}" in
    ""|none) printf 'fiz\n' ;;
    anthropic) printf 'claude\n' ;;
    *)       printf '%s\n' "$1" ;;
  esac
}

# Mint a fresh cell-id: UTC timestamp + 4 random hex chars.
new_cell_id() {
  local ts rand
  ts="$(date -u +%Y%m%dT%H%M%SZ)"
  rand="$(LC_ALL=C tr -dc 'a-f0-9' </dev/urandom 2>/dev/null | head -c 4 || true)"
  if [[ -z "${rand}" || ${#rand} -lt 4 ]]; then
    rand="$(printf '%04x' $((RANDOM & 0xFFFF)))"
  fi
  printf '%s-%s\n' "${ts}" "${rand}"
}

# task_executor_version: sha256 of the harbor task-executor script. The
# task-executor is shell, so the script bytes are the version of record.
task_executor_version() {
  local f="${TASK_EXECUTORS_DIR}/harbor"
  if [[ -f "${f}" ]]; then
    sha256sum "${f}" | awk '{print $1}'
  else
    printf 'unknown\n'
  fi
}

# harbor_runner_image_digest: docker image Id for fizeau-harbor-runner. When
# docker is absent (CI / dry-run), fall back to the source-content sha so the
# sweep manifest is still well-defined.
harbor_runner_image_digest() {
  # Test/operator override wins so suites can pin the recorded digest.
  if [[ -n "${BENCH_HARBOR_DIGEST_OVERRIDE:-}" ]]; then
    printf '%s\n' "${BENCH_HARBOR_DIGEST_OVERRIDE}"
    return 0
  fi
  local tag="${HARBOR_IMAGE_TAG}"
  if command -v docker >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    local d
    d="$(docker image inspect --format '{{.Id}}' "${tag}" 2>/dev/null || true)"
    if [[ -n "${d}" ]]; then
      printf '%s\n' "${d}"
      return 0
    fi
  fi
  # No docker (or image absent). Derive from the source-content sha used by
  # preflight.
  if compute_harbor_source_sha >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    printf 'src:%s\n' "$(compute_harbor_source_sha)"
  else
    printf 'unknown\n'
  fi
}

# Write <out>/sweep.json with the executor + image identifiers. Called once
# per ./benchmark invocation; each cell's report.json embeds the same values.
write_sweep_metadata() {
  local out="$1" te_ver="$2" hd="$3"
  mkdir -p "${out}"
  local started
  started="$(date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ)"
  jq -n \
    --arg te_ver "${te_ver}" \
    --arg hd "${hd}" \
    --arg started "${started}" \
    '{task_executor_version:$te_ver, harbor_runner_image_digest:$hd, started_at:$started}' \
    >"${out}/sweep.json.tmp"
  mv "${out}/sweep.json.tmp" "${out}/sweep.json"
}

# compose_env_redacted prints command-spec env with secret_env_keys replaced
# by a redaction sentinel. Order-insensitive; entries missing from env are
# ignored.
compose_env_redacted() {
  local env_json="$1" secret_keys_json="$2"
  jq -c --argjson secrets "${secret_keys_json}" '
    . as $env
    | reduce ($secrets[]? | tostring) as $k
        ($env; if has($k) then .[$k] = "***REDACTED***" else . end)
  ' <<<"${env_json}"
}

# is_transient_stderr greps the executor's stderr for known transient error
# signatures: connection refused/reset, HTTP 5xx, unexpected/EOF parse.
is_transient_stderr() {
  local f="$1"
  [[ -s "${f}" ]] || return 1
  LC_ALL=C grep -qE 'connection refused|connection reset|HTTP 5[0-9][0-9]|status[ =:]?5[0-9][0-9]|status_code=5[0-9][0-9]|unexpected EOF|EOF parse|EOF while|temporarily unavailable' "${f}"
}

# Invoke the task-executor with bounded exponential backoff on transient
# errors. Returns 0 on success; non-zero on terminal failure after retries.
# Captures attempts log to ${cell_dir}/retry-log.txt for forensics.
run_executor_with_retry() {
  local task_spec="$1" cell_dir="$2" executor="$3"
  local stderr_file
  stderr_file="$(mktemp)"
  local max_attempts="${BENCH_RETRY_MAX_ATTEMPTS}"
  local backoff="${BENCH_RETRY_BACKOFF_BASE}"
  local attempt=1
  local status=0
  local retry_log="${cell_dir}/retry-log.txt"
  : >"${retry_log}"
  while :; do
    : >"${stderr_file}"
    set +e
    printf '%s' "${task_spec}" | "${executor}" 2>"${stderr_file}" >/dev/null
    status=$?
    set -e
    printf 'attempt=%d status=%d\n' "${attempt}" "${status}" >>"${retry_log}"
    if [[ ${status} -eq 0 ]]; then
      rm -f "${stderr_file}"
      return 0
    fi
    cat "${stderr_file}" >>"${retry_log}"
    if (( attempt > max_attempts )) || ! is_transient_stderr "${stderr_file}"; then
      cat "${stderr_file}" >&2
      rm -f "${stderr_file}"
      return "${status}"
    fi
    warn "cell $(basename "${cell_dir}"): transient error (attempt ${attempt}); sleeping ${backoff}s"
    sleep "${backoff}"
    backoff=$((backoff * 2))
    attempt=$((attempt + 1))
  done
}

# resolve_tasks_dir derives the host tasks dir for terminal-bench cells.
# Honors BENCH_TASKS_DIR override (used by tests).
resolve_tasks_dir() {
  local framework="$1"
  if [[ -n "${BENCH_TASKS_DIR:-}" ]]; then
    printf '%s\n' "${BENCH_TASKS_DIR}"
    return 0
  fi
  case "${framework}" in
    terminal-bench) printf '%s\n' "${BENCH_TASKS_DIR_DEFAULT}" ;;
    *)              printf '%s\n' "${BENCH_TASKS_DIR_DEFAULT}" ;;
  esac
}

# Count existing terminal cells matching (profile_id, task) under <out>; also
# emit lists of invalid cells and orphan cells (cells with cell-state.json
# and no terminal report.json). Lines are written to the named output paths.
inspect_existing_cells() {
  local out="$1" dataset_dir="$2" task="$3" profile_id="$4"
  local terminal_out="$5" invalid_out="$6" orphan_out="$7"
  : >"${terminal_out}"
  : >"${invalid_out}"
  : >"${orphan_out}"
  local task_dir="${out}/cells/${dataset_dir}/${task}"
  [[ -d "${task_dir}" ]] || return 0
  local cell
  shopt -s nullglob
  for cell in "${task_dir}"/*/; do
    local cdir="${cell%/}"
    local report="${cdir}/report.json"
    local state="${cdir}/cell-state.json"
    if [[ -f "${report}" ]]; then
      local pid status inv
      pid="$(jq -r '.profile.id // ""' "${report}" 2>/dev/null || true)"
      [[ "${pid}" == "${profile_id}" ]] || continue
      status="$(jq -r '.final_status // ""' "${report}" 2>/dev/null || true)"
      inv="$(jq -r '.invalid_class // ""' "${report}" 2>/dev/null || true)"
      if [[ -n "${inv}" ]]; then
        printf '%s\n' "${cdir}" >>"${invalid_out}"
        continue
      fi
      if is_terminal_status "${status}"; then
        printf '%s\n' "${cdir}" >>"${terminal_out}"
      fi
      continue
    fi
    if [[ -f "${state}" ]]; then
      local pid
      pid="$(jq -r '.profile // ""' "${state}" 2>/dev/null || true)"
      [[ -z "${pid}" || "${pid}" == "${profile_id}" ]] || continue
      printf '%s\n' "${cdir}" >>"${orphan_out}"
    fi
  done
  shopt -u nullglob
}

# compute_cell_cost prints the cost_usd_at_run_time for a single cell. Uses
# profile.pricing × token streams pulled from the cell's result.json. Token
# field names follow the canonical report-level naming
# (input_tokens / output_tokens / cached_input_tokens) and are also probed
# under the harbor totals naming (total_prompt_tokens / total_completion_tokens
# / total_cached_input_tokens) so harness-native token reporters land in the
# same accumulator.
#
# When profile.pricing is all zero (or unset) and the executor wrote an
# explicit cost_usd / total_cost_usd in result.json, the explicit value is
# honored — that lets harnesses that observe API-reported cost directly
# participate in budget enforcement.
compute_cell_cost() {
  local result_json="${1:-}" profile_json="${2:-}"
  [[ -n "${result_json}" ]] || result_json='{}'
  [[ -n "${profile_json}" ]] || profile_json='{}'
  jq -nr \
    --argjson result "${result_json}" \
    --argjson profile "${profile_json}" \
    '
      def num(v): if (v == null) then 0 else (v | tonumber? // 0) end;
      ($profile.pricing // {}) as $p
      | num($p.input_usd_per_mtok)         as $pi
      | num($p.output_usd_per_mtok)        as $po
      | num($p.cached_input_usd_per_mtok)  as $pc
      | num($result.input_tokens
            // $result.total_prompt_tokens
            // $result.n_input_tokens
            // 0)                          as $ti
      | num($result.output_tokens
            // $result.total_completion_tokens
            // $result.n_output_tokens
            // 0)                          as $to
      | num($result.cached_input_tokens
            // $result.total_cached_input_tokens
            // $result.n_cached_input_tokens
            // 0)                          as $tc
      | num($result.cost_usd // $result.total_cost_usd // 0) as $reported
      | (($ti * $pi) + ($to * $po) + ($tc * $pc)) / 1000000  as $priced
      | (if $priced > 0 then $priced
         elif ($pi + $po + $pc) == 0 then $reported
         else 0
         end)
      | tostring
    '
}

#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Integration test for the ./benchmark run path (bead fizeau-a2fd070b).
#
# Exercises all five ACs of the bead description:
#
#  1. ./benchmark --profile sindri-lucebox --bench-set tb-2-1-canary produces
#     cells with report.json embedding profile, command, env_redacted +
#     fiz.txt, fiz.err, session/ artifacts.
#  2. <out>/sweep.json captures task_executor_version +
#     harbor_runner_image_digest; each cell's report.json references them.
#  3. Re-running without --force-rerun skips cells with terminal report.json.
#  4. --retry-invalid reruns invalid cells; new cell links via attempt_of and
#     prior cell receives superseded_by back-link.
#  5. Transient 5xx triggers bounded exponential backoff + eventual success.
#
# Uses HARBOR_TASK_EXECUTOR_DRY_RUN=1 (the harbor task-executor's documented
# dry-run mode) for ACs 1–4 so docker is not required. AC5 swaps in a stub
# executor (BENCH_TASK_EXECUTOR_OVERRIDE) that emits 5xx for the first few
# attempts and then succeeds, to drive the retry loop deterministically.

set -euo pipefail

SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
BENCHMARK="${SCRIPT_DIR}/benchmark"

TMP_ROOT=""
cleanup() {
  if [[ -n "${TMP_ROOT}" && -d "${TMP_ROOT}" ]]; then
    rm -rf "${TMP_ROOT}"
  fi
}
trap cleanup EXIT

fail() {
  echo "FAIL: $*" >&2
  exit 1
}

assert_eq() {
  local got="$1" want="$2" msg="${3:-values differ}"
  [[ "${got}" == "${want}" ]] || fail "${msg}: got=${got} want=${want}"
}

assert_nonempty() {
  local v="$1" msg="${2:-value is empty}"
  [[ -n "${v}" ]] || fail "${msg}"
}

require() {
  command -v "$1" >/dev/null 2>&1 || fail "required tool not found: $1"
}

require jq
require yq
require sha256sum

TMP_ROOT="$(mktemp -d)"
TASKS_DIR="${TMP_ROOT}/tasks"
OUT_DIR="${TMP_ROOT}/out"
STUB_DIR="${TMP_ROOT}/stubs"
mkdir -p "${TASKS_DIR}" "${OUT_DIR}" "${STUB_DIR}"

PROFILE="sindri-lucebox"
BENCH_SET="tb-2-1-canary"
DATASET="terminal-bench-2-1"
TASKS=(cancel-async-tasks log-summary-date-ranges configure-git-webserver)

echo "==> AC1+AC2: initial sweep produces cells + sweep.json"

env HARBOR_TASK_EXECUTOR_DRY_RUN=1 \
    BENCH_TASKS_DIR="${TASKS_DIR}" \
    BENCH_HARBOR_DIGEST_OVERRIDE="sha256:test-digest-aaa" \
    "${BENCHMARK}" \
      --profile "${PROFILE}" \
      --bench-set "${BENCH_SET}" \
      --reps 1 \
      --out "${OUT_DIR}" \
    >/dev/null

# sweep.json present with both fields
sweep_json="${OUT_DIR}/sweep.json"
[[ -f "${sweep_json}" ]] || fail "sweep.json missing at ${sweep_json}"
te_ver="$(jq -r '.task_executor_version // ""' "${sweep_json}")"
hd="$(jq -r '.harbor_runner_image_digest // ""' "${sweep_json}")"
assert_nonempty "${te_ver}" "sweep.json: task_executor_version empty"
assert_nonempty "${hd}"     "sweep.json: harbor_runner_image_digest empty"
assert_eq "${hd}" "sha256:test-digest-aaa" "sweep.json: digest mismatch"

# One cell per task
for task in "${TASKS[@]}"; do
  task_dir="${OUT_DIR}/cells/${DATASET}/${task}"
  [[ -d "${task_dir}" ]] || fail "cells dir missing for task=${task}: ${task_dir}"
  cells=("${task_dir}"/*/)
  [[ ${#cells[@]} -eq 1 ]] || fail "task=${task}: expected 1 cell, got ${#cells[@]}"
  cell="${cells[0]%/}"
  report="${cell}/report.json"
  [[ -f "${report}" ]] || fail "task=${task}: report.json missing at ${report}"

  # report.json embedded fields
  [[ "$(jq -r '.profile.id // ""' "${report}")" == "${PROFILE}" ]] \
    || fail "task=${task}: report.json profile.id != ${PROFILE}"
  jq -e '.command | type == "array"' "${report}" >/dev/null \
    || fail "task=${task}: report.json missing command array"
  jq -e '.env_redacted | type == "object"' "${report}" >/dev/null \
    || fail "task=${task}: report.json missing env_redacted object"
  jq -e '.task_executor_version' "${report}" >/dev/null \
    || fail "task=${task}: report.json missing task_executor_version"
  jq -e '.harbor_runner_image_digest' "${report}" >/dev/null \
    || fail "task=${task}: report.json missing harbor_runner_image_digest"
  assert_eq "$(jq -r '.task_executor_version' "${report}")" "${te_ver}" \
    "task=${task}: report.json task_executor_version mismatch"
  assert_eq "$(jq -r '.harbor_runner_image_digest' "${report}")" "${hd}" \
    "task=${task}: report.json harbor_runner_image_digest mismatch"

  # Secret redaction: FIZEAU_API_KEY is flagged secret in fiz adapter output
  redacted="$(jq -r '.env_redacted.FIZEAU_API_KEY // ""' "${report}")"
  assert_eq "${redacted}" "***REDACTED***" \
    "task=${task}: FIZEAU_API_KEY should be redacted in env_redacted"

  # Sibling artifacts
  [[ -f "${cell}/fiz.txt" ]] || fail "task=${task}: fiz.txt missing"
  [[ -f "${cell}/fiz.err" ]] || fail "task=${task}: fiz.err missing"
  [[ -d "${cell}/session" ]] || fail "task=${task}: session/ missing"

  # cell-state.json was cleaned up on terminal close
  [[ ! -f "${cell}/cell-state.json" ]] \
    || fail "task=${task}: cell-state.json should be deleted on terminal close"

  # final_status is terminal
  status="$(jq -r '.final_status // ""' "${report}")"
  case "${status}" in
    completed|pass|fail|timeout) ;;
    *) fail "task=${task}: unexpected final_status=${status}" ;;
  esac
done

echo "==> AC3: rerun (no flag) skips terminal cells"

before_count="$(find "${OUT_DIR}/cells" -mindepth 3 -maxdepth 3 -type d | wc -l | tr -d ' ')"
env HARBOR_TASK_EXECUTOR_DRY_RUN=1 \
    BENCH_TASKS_DIR="${TASKS_DIR}" \
    BENCH_HARBOR_DIGEST_OVERRIDE="sha256:test-digest-aaa" \
    "${BENCHMARK}" \
      --profile "${PROFILE}" \
      --bench-set "${BENCH_SET}" \
      --reps 1 \
      --out "${OUT_DIR}" \
    >/dev/null
after_count="$(find "${OUT_DIR}/cells" -mindepth 3 -maxdepth 3 -type d | wc -l | tr -d ' ')"
assert_eq "${after_count}" "${before_count}" "resume default added cells (${before_count} -> ${after_count})"

echo "==> AC4: --retry-invalid reruns invalid cells with attempt_of + superseded_by"

# Mark the cancel-async-tasks cell as invalid by injecting invalid_class.
prior_cell="$(find "${OUT_DIR}/cells/${DATASET}/cancel-async-tasks" -mindepth 1 -maxdepth 1 -type d | head -n1)"
[[ -n "${prior_cell}" ]] || fail "no prior cell found for cancel-async-tasks"
prior_report="${prior_cell}/report.json"
tmp_report="${TMP_ROOT}/prior_report.json"
jq '.invalid_class = "setup_failure" | .final_status = "invalid"' "${prior_report}" >"${tmp_report}"
mv "${tmp_report}" "${prior_report}"
prior_cell_id="$(jq -r '.cell_id' "${prior_report}")"

env HARBOR_TASK_EXECUTOR_DRY_RUN=1 \
    BENCH_TASKS_DIR="${TASKS_DIR}" \
    BENCH_HARBOR_DIGEST_OVERRIDE="sha256:test-digest-aaa" \
    "${BENCHMARK}" \
      --profile "${PROFILE}" \
      --bench-set "${BENCH_SET}" \
      --reps 1 \
      --retry-invalid \
      --out "${OUT_DIR}" \
    >/dev/null

# A new cell should now exist whose attempt_of points at the prior cell.
new_cell=""
for c in "${OUT_DIR}/cells/${DATASET}/cancel-async-tasks"/*/; do
  cdir="${c%/}"
  [[ "${cdir}" == "${prior_cell}" ]] && continue
  if [[ -f "${cdir}/report.json" ]]; then
    ao="$(jq -r '.attempt_of // ""' "${cdir}/report.json")"
    if [[ "${ao}" == "${prior_cell}" ]]; then
      new_cell="${cdir}"
      break
    fi
  fi
done
[[ -n "${new_cell}" ]] || fail "retry-invalid: no new cell with attempt_of pointing at ${prior_cell}"

new_cell_id="$(jq -r '.cell_id' "${new_cell}/report.json")"
prior_superseded="$(jq -r '.superseded_by // ""' "${prior_report}")"
assert_eq "${prior_superseded}" "${new_cell_id}" "prior cell missing superseded_by back-link"
echo "    prior=${prior_cell_id} superseded_by=${new_cell_id}"

# Orphan cell-state.json under --retry-invalid: create one, rerun, verify
# new cell links to it via attempt_of.
orphan_dir="${OUT_DIR}/cells/${DATASET}/log-summary-date-ranges/19700101T000000Z-dead"
mkdir -p "${orphan_dir}"
jq -n --arg p "${PROFILE}" --arg t "log-summary-date-ranges" \
  '{cell_id:"19700101T000000Z-dead", task:$t, profile:$p, started_at:"1970-01-01T00:00:00Z", status:"running"}' \
  >"${orphan_dir}/cell-state.json"

env HARBOR_TASK_EXECUTOR_DRY_RUN=1 \
    BENCH_TASKS_DIR="${TASKS_DIR}" \
    BENCH_HARBOR_DIGEST_OVERRIDE="sha256:test-digest-aaa" \
    "${BENCHMARK}" \
      --profile "${PROFILE}" \
      --bench-set "${BENCH_SET}" \
      --reps 1 \
      --retry-invalid \
      --out "${OUT_DIR}" \
    >/dev/null

orphan_report="${orphan_dir}/report.json"
[[ -f "${orphan_report}" ]] || fail "orphan cell did not receive back-written report.json"
orphan_super="$(jq -r '.superseded_by // ""' "${orphan_report}")"
assert_nonempty "${orphan_super}" "orphan cell missing superseded_by"

found_orphan_retry=0
for c in "${OUT_DIR}/cells/${DATASET}/log-summary-date-ranges"/*/; do
  cdir="${c%/}"

                "error": {
                    "message": 'reasoning="low" is not supported by provider type "llama-server"'

            }).encode())
            return

  matrix-index)
    exit 0
    ;;
  *)
    exit 0
    ;;
esac
BENCH
else
  cat > "${out}" <<'FIZ'
#!/usr/bin/env bash
exit 0
FIZ
fi
chmod +x "${out}"
SH
  chmod +x "${bin_dir}/go"
}
